831	  %  induction on $n$ (applied to the data $A_g$, $B_g$, $T$),"
832	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (support-dimension drop).}
833	  Let \(A\) be a domain, \(n \ge 0\), and \(0 \ne F \in A[X_0, \dots, X_n]\). Then there
834	  exist a triangular \(A\)-algebra automorphism \(e\) of \(A[X_0, \dots, X_n]\) and a
835	  non-zero \(g \in A\) such that, after inverting \(g\), the image of \(e(F)\) in
836	  \(A_g[X_0, \dots, X_n]\), viewed as a univariate polynomial in \(X_0\) over
837	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n]\), has a \emph{unit} leading coefficient. Equivalently, \(e(F)\)
838	  is monic in \(X_0\) up to the unit \(g\) over \(A_g\).
839	\end{lemma}
840	
841	\begin{proof}
842	
843	\leanok
844	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1766-L1768).
845	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (support-dimension drop, Nagata normalisation).}
846	  Apply the Nagata substitution \(X_i \mapsto X_i + X_0^{r^{\,i}}\) for \(i = 1, \dots, n\)
847	  and \(X_0 \mapsto X_0\), with \(r\) larger than every exponent occurring in \(F\): it is a
848	  triangular (hence invertible) \(A\)-algebra automorphism \(e\) of \(A[X_0, \dots, X_n]\).
849	  Under this substitution the distinct monomials of \(F\) acquire distinct degrees in
850	  \(X_0\), so \(e(F)\), expanded as a polynomial in \(X_0\), has a unique top-degree term
851	  whose coefficient is the coefficient \(c \in A\) of the top total-degree monomial of
852	  \(F\); since \(F \ne 0\) this \(c \ne 0\). Put \(g := c\). The degree of \(e(F)\) in
853	  \(X_0\) equals the total degree of \(F\), and its leading coefficient in \(X_0\) is the
854	  constant \(g\). Passing to \(A_g = A[g^{-1}]\), the image of \(g\) is a unit, so the
855	  image of \(e(F)\) in \(A_g[X_0, \dots, X_n]\) is monic in \(X_0\) up to the unit \(g\).
856	\end{proof}

1512	  affine flat-base-change comparison invertible without inspecting its concrete sheaf assembly.

1515	\begin{lemma}[The iterated mate of a left-adjoint square is the conjugate (Beck--Chevalley)]

1518	  \mathlibok

1521	  \(\mathrm{adj}_1, \dots, \mathrm{adj}_4\), the iterated mate (the Beck--Chevalley \(2\)-cell of the
1522	  pasted square) of a \(2\)-cell \(\alpha\) equals the conjugate of \(\alpha\) for the two composite
1523	  adjunctions:
1524	  \[

1526	      \operatorname{conj}\bigl(\mathrm{adj}_1.\mathrm{adj}_4,\ \mathrm{adj}_3.\mathrm{adj}_2\bigr)\,\alpha .
1527	  \]

1529	  transformation is a natural isomorphism exactly when \(\alpha\) is — the conceptual home of the
1530	  iso-ness of the affine comparison \(g^* f_*\widetilde M \to f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\), which the
1531	  project realises directly as a conjugate of the pushforward \(\Gamma\)-comparison.

1665	[Pseudofunctoriality of the pullback--pushforward unit]
1666	  \label{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}
1667	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}
1668	  \uses{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib, lem:comp_unit_app_mathlib,
1669	        lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}
1670	  Let \(a : X_1 \to X_2\) and \(b : X_2 \to X_3\) be composable morphisms of schemes and let
1671	  \(N\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_{X_3}\)-module. Write \(\eta^{h}\) for the unit of the
1672	  pullback--pushforward adjunction \((h^{*}\dashv h_{*})\) attached to a morphism \(h\), and
1673	  \(\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b}\), \(\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b}\) for the pseudofunctor
1674	  composition coherences. Then
1675	  \[
1676	    \eta^{b}_{N} \;\circ\; b_{*}\bigl(\eta^{a}_{\,b^{*}N}\bigr)
1677	      \;\circ\; (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{hom}}_{N}
1678	    \;=\;
1679	    \eta^{a\circ b}_{N} \;\circ\;
1680	      (a\circ b)_{*}\bigl((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}_{N}\bigr),
1681	  \]
1682	  where composites are read left to right. In words: the unit of the composite morphism
1683	  \(a \circ b\), reindexed by the inverse pullback coherence, factors as the \(b\)-unit, followed
1684	  by \(b_{*}\) of the \(a\)-unit, followed by the pushforward coherence. This is the leg-reindex
1685	  engine consumed by Seam~2 (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}): with \(a\) an
1686	  isomorphism and \(b\) an affine \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-map it turns the generic projection-leg
1687	  unit into the affine \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-unit modulo the transparent coherences.

1691	        lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}
1692	  Apply the unit-across-conjugate coherence Lemma~\ref{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib} to the
1693	  composite adjunction \((b^{*}\dashv b_{*}) . (a^{*}\dashv a_{*})\), the adjunction
1694	  \(\bigl((a\circ b)^{*}\dashv (a\circ b)_{*}\bigr)\), and the left-adjoint transformation
1695	  \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}\). This yields an identity whose right-hand factor
1696	  is the conjugate (mate) of \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}\); by

4267	[The structure-sheaf restriction ring map \texorpdfstring{$A \to \Gamma(U,\mathcal{O}_X)$}{A to sections over U}]
4268	  \label{def:fbcb_rhoU}
4269	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rhoU}
4270	  \uses{def:fbcb_groundRing}
4271	  For an open \(U \subseteq X\), the structure-sheaf restriction along \(U \subseteq \top\) is a ring
4272	  homomorphism \(\rho_U : A = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X) \to \Gamma(U,\mathcal{O}_X)\). It is the ring map
4273	  along which sections over \(U\) are regarded as \(A\)-modules.
4274	\end{definition}
4275	
4276	\begin{lemma}\leanok
4277	[Transitivity of the restriction ring maps]
4278	  \label{lem:fbcb_rhoU_comp}
4279	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rhoU_comp}
4280	  \uses{def:fbcb_rhoU}
4281	  For opens \(V \le U \subseteq X\), the structure-sheaf restriction
4282	  \(\Gamma(U,\mathcal{O}_X) \to \Gamma(V,\mathcal{O}_X)\) composed with \(\rho_U\) equals \(\rho_V\):
4283	  \[
4284	    \bigl(\Gamma(U,\mathcal{O}_X) \to \Gamma(V,\mathcal{O}_X)\bigr) \circ \rho_U = \rho_V .
4285	  \]
4286	  \begin{proof}
4287	    \uses{def:fbcb_rhoU}
4288	    Both sides are the structure-sheaf restriction \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X) \to \Gamma(V,\mathcal{O}_X)\)
4289	    along \(V \subseteq \top\), factored as \(V \subseteq U \subseteq \top\); functoriality of the
4290	    structure presheaf collapses the two-step restriction to the one-step restriction.
4291	  \end{proof}
4292	\end{lemma}
4293	
4294	\begin{definition}\leanok
4295	[Sections over \texorpdfstring{$U$}{U} as an \texorpdfstring{$A$}{A}-module]
4296	  \label{def:fbcb_gammaModA}
4297	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaModA}
4298	  \uses{def:fbcb_rhoU}
4299	  The sections \(\Gamma(U, M)\), a module over \(\Gamma(U,\mathcal{O}_X)\), are regarded as an
4300	  \(A\)-module by restriction of scalars along \(\rho_U\) (Definition~\ref{def:fbcb_rhoU}). This is the
4301	  common \(A\)-module home in which the global-sections equalizer is formed.
4302	\end{definition}
4303	
4304	\begin{definition}\leanok
4305	[The restriction \texorpdfstring{$\Gamma(U,M) \to \Gamma(V,M)$}{Gamma(U,M) to Gamma(V,M)} as an \texorpdfstring{$A$}{A}-module morphism]
4306	  \label{def:fbcb_gammaResAHom}
4307	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaResAHom}
4308	  \uses{def:fbcb_gammaModA, lem:fbcb_rhoU_comp}
4309	  For \(V \le U\), the structure-sheaf restriction \(M.\mathrm{map} : \Gamma(U,M) \to \Gamma(V,M)\) is
4310	  promoted to a morphism of \(A\)-modules \(\Gamma(U,M) \to \Gamma(V,M)\) (Definition~\ref{def:fbcb_gammaModA}).
4311	  It is built from \(M.\mathrm{map}\) by restriction of scalars, using the transitivity
4312	  \(\rho\)-coherence Lemma~\ref{lem:fbcb_rhoU_comp} to identify the two scalar actions.
4313	\end{definition}
4314	
4315	\begin{definition}\leanok
4316	[The \texorpdfstring{$A$}{A}-linear restriction map]
4317	  \label{def:fbcb_gammaResA}
4318	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaResA}
4319	  \uses{def:fbcb_gammaResAHom}
4320	  The underlying \(A\)-linear map \(\Gamma(U,M) \to_A \Gamma(V,M)\) of the \(A\)-module morphism of
4321	  Definition~\ref{def:fbcb_gammaResAHom}. It is the building block of the two product legs of the
4322	  equalizer.
4323	\end{definition}
4324	
4325	\begin{lemma}\leanok
4326	[The \texorpdfstring{$A$}{A}-linear restriction is the underlying presheaf restriction]
4327	  \label{lem:fbcb_gammaResA_apply}
4328	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaResA_apply}
4329	  \uses{def:fbcb_gammaResA}
4330	  On underlying elements, the \(A\)-linear restriction map of Definition~\ref{def:fbcb_gammaResA} acts
4331	  as the presheaf restriction of \(M\): for \(x \in \Gamma(U,M)\) and \(V \le U\),
4332	  \(\mathrm{gammaResA}(x) = (M.\mathrm{map})\,x\).
4333	  \begin{proof}
4334	    \uses{def:fbcb_gammaResA}
4335	    Restriction of scalars and the composition with the comparison isomorphism of
4336	    Definition~\ref{def:fbcb_gammaResAHom} leave the underlying function unchanged, so the map agrees
4337	    with \(M.\mathrm{map}\) on elements.
4338	  \end{proof}
4339	\end{lemma}
4340	
4341	\begin{lemma}\leanok
4342	[Functoriality of the \texorpdfstring{$A$}{A}-linear restriction maps]
4343	  \label{lem:fbcb_gammaResA_comp}
4344	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaResA_comp}
4345	  \uses{def:fbcb_gammaResA, lem:fbcb_gammaResA_apply}
4346	  For opens \(W \le V \le U\) and \(x \in \Gamma(U,M)\), the \(A\)-linear restrictions compose as the
4347	  single restriction:
4348	  \[
4349	    \mathrm{gammaResA}_{V \le U}\!\bigl(x\bigr) \text{ then } \mathrm{gammaResA}_{W \le V}
4350	      \;=\; \mathrm{gammaResA}_{W \le U}\!\bigl(x\bigr).
4351	  \]
4352	  \begin{proof}
4353	    \uses{def:fbcb_gammaResA, lem:fbcb_gammaResA_apply}
4354	    By Lemma~\ref{lem:fbcb_gammaResA_apply} each \(A\)-linear restriction is the underlying presheaf
4355	    restriction of \(M\); functoriality of the presheaf \(M\) collapses the composite of the two
4356	    restrictions \(W \subseteq V \subseteq U\) to the single restriction \(W \subseteq U\).
4357	  \end{proof}
4358	\end{lemma}
4359	
4360	\begin{definition}\leanok
4361	[The left restriction leg]
4362	  \label{def:fbcb_leftRes}
4363	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.leftRes}
4364	  \uses{def:fbcb_gammaResA}
4365	  The \(A\)-linear map
4366	  \[
4367	    \mathrm{leftRes} : \prod_i \Gamma(U_i, M) \longrightarrow \prod_{(i,j)} \Gamma(U_i \cap U_j, M),
4368	  \]
4369	  whose \((i,j)\)-component is the \(A\)-linear restriction \(\Gamma(U_i, M) \to \Gamma(U_i \cap U_j, M)\)
4370	  of Definition~\ref{def:fbcb_gammaResA} applied to the \(i\)-th factor (restriction along
4371	  \(U_i \cap U_j \subseteq U_i\)).
4372	\end{definition}
4373	
4374	\begin{definition}\leanok
4375	[The right restriction leg]
4376	  \label{def:fbcb_rightRes}
4377	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rightRes}
4378	  \uses{def:fbcb_gammaResA}
4379	  The \(A\)-linear map \(\mathrm{rightRes} : \prod_i \Gamma(U_i, M) \to \prod_{(i,j)} \Gamma(U_i \cap U_j, M)\)
4380	  whose \((i,j)\)-component is the \(A\)-linear restriction of Definition~\ref{def:fbcb_gammaResA} applied
4381	  to the \(j\)-th factor (restriction along \(U_i \cap U_j \subseteq U_j\)).
4382	\end{definition}
4383	
4384	\begin{definition}\leanok
4385	[Restriction of a global section to the cover]
4386	  \label{def:fbcb_toCover}
4387	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.toCover}
4388	  \uses{def:fbcb_gammaResA}
4389	  The \(A\)-linear map \(\mathrm{toCover} : \Gamma(\top, M) \to \prod_i \Gamma(U_i, M)\) restricting a
4390	  global section to each member of the cover, with \(i\)-th component the \(A\)-linear restriction
4391	  \(\Gamma(\top, M) \to \Gamma(U_i, M)\) of Definition~\ref{def:fbcb_gammaResA}.
4392	\end{definition}
4393	
4394	\begin{lemma}\leanok
4395	[The restricted family is compatible]
4396	  \label{lem:fbcb_leftRes_toCover}
4397	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.leftRes_toCover}
4398	  \uses{def:fbcb_leftRes, def:fbcb_rightRes, def:fbcb_toCover, lem:fbcb_gammaResA_comp}
4399	  For every global section \(s \in \Gamma(\top, M)\) the two legs agree on the restricted family:
4400	  \[
4401	    \mathrm{leftRes}\bigl(\mathrm{toCover}(s)\bigr) = \mathrm{rightRes}\bigl(\mathrm{toCover}(s)\bigr).
4402	  \]
4403	  \begin{proof}
4404	    \uses{def:fbcb_leftRes, def:fbcb_rightRes, def:fbcb_toCover, lem:fbcb_gammaResA_comp}
4405	    Both sides are, in the \((i,j)\)-component, the restriction of \(s\) from \(\top\) to
4406	    \(U_i \cap U_j\): the left leg restricts \(\top \to U_i \to U_i \cap U_j\) and the right leg
4407	    restricts \(\top \to U_j \to U_i \cap U_j\), and by the functoriality
4408	    Lemma~\ref{lem:fbcb_gammaResA_comp} each two-step restriction collapses to the one-step restriction
4409	    \(\top \to U_i \cap U_j\). Hence the two components coincide.
4410	  \end{proof}
4411	\end{lemma}
4412	
4413	\begin{definition}\leanok
4414	[The corestriction into the equalizer locus]
4415	  \label{def:fbcb_toCoverEqLocus}
4416	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.toCoverEqLocus}
4417	  \uses{def:fbcb_toCover, lem:fbcb_leftRes_toCover}
4418	  By the compatibility Lemma~\ref{lem:fbcb_leftRes_toCover}, the map \(\mathrm{toCover}\) corestricts to
4419	  an \(A\)-linear map
4420	  \[
4421	    \mathrm{toCoverEqLocus} : \Gamma(\top, M)
4422	      \longrightarrow \operatorname{eqLocus}(\mathrm{leftRes}, \mathrm{rightRes})
4423	      = \{\, t \in \textstyle\prod_i \Gamma(U_i,M) : \mathrm{leftRes}(t) = \mathrm{rightRes}(t)\,\}.
4424	  \]
4425	\end{definition}
4426	
4427	\begin{lemma}\leanok
4428	[Global sections as an \texorpdfstring{$A$}{A}-module equalizer locus]
4429	  \label{lem:fbcb_gammaTopEquivEqLocus}
4430	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaTopEquivEqLocus}
4431	  \uses{def:fbcb_toCoverEqLocus, def:fbcb_gammaModA, def:fbcb_gammaResA, def:fbcb_leftRes,
4432	        def:fbcb_rightRes, lem:sheaf_eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
4433	  % NOTE: bijectivity is established by the ELEMENT-LEVEL sheaf axioms
4434	  % (lem:sheaf_eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib for injectivity, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib for
4435	  % surjectivity) on the underlying Ab-sheaf M.presheaf; it does NOT route through the
4436	  % sheaf-condition-fork limit (lem:sheaf_equalizer_products_mathlib / gammaIsLimitSheafConditionFork).
4437	  For \(M : X.\mathrm{Modules}\) and any open cover \(U\) of \(X\) (\(\bigvee_i U_i = \top\)), the
4438	  corestriction \(\mathrm{toCoverEqLocus}\) of Definition~\ref{def:fbcb_toCoverEqLocus} is an
4439	  \(A\)-linear isomorphism
4440	  \[
4441	    \Gamma(X, M) = \Gamma(\top, M) \;\cong\; \operatorname{eqLocus}(\mathrm{leftRes}, \mathrm{rightRes}),
4442	  \]
4443	  identifying the global sections, as a module over the ground ring \(A = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\), with
4444	  the submodule on which the two restriction legs agree.
4445	  \begin{proof}
4446	    \uses{def:fbcb_toCoverEqLocus, lem:sheaf_eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib,
4447	          lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
4448	    The map \(\mathrm{toCoverEqLocus}\) is \(A\)-linear by construction, so it suffices to prove it
4449	    bijective on underlying sets. \emph{Injectivity:} if two global sections \(s, t\) have equal images,
4450	    their restrictions to every \(U_i\) agree, so by sheaf separatedness
4451	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:sheaf_eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}) applied to the underlying abelian-group sheaf
4452	    \(M.\mathrm{presheaf}\), \(s = t\). \emph{Surjectivity:} an element of the locus is a family
4453	    \((s_i)_i\) with \(\mathrm{leftRes} = \mathrm{rightRes}\), i.e.\ a compatible family on the cover; by
4454	    the gluing axiom (Lemma~\ref{lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}) it amalgamates to a global
4455	    section restricting to \((s_i)_i\). Hence \(\mathrm{toCoverEqLocus}\) is an \(A\)-linear bijection,
4456	    so an isomorphism.
4457	  \end{proof}
4458	\end{lemma}
4459	
4460	\begin{lemma}\leanok
4461	[Flat base change commutes with the \texorpdfstring{$H^0$}{H0} equalizer]
4462	  \label{lem:fbcb_baseChangeGammaEquiv}
4463	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.baseChangeGammaEquiv}
4464	  \uses{lem:fbcb_gammaTopEquivEqLocus, lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
4465	  Let \(B\) be a flat \(A\)-algebra (\(A = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\)). Then base changing the global
4466	  sections along \(A \to B\) is the equalizer of the base-changed restriction legs: there is a
4467	  \(B\)-linear isomorphism
4468	  \[
4469	    B \otimes_A \Gamma(X, M) \;\cong\;
4470	      \operatorname{eqLocus}\bigl(\mathrm{id}_B \otimes \mathrm{leftRes},\ \mathrm{id}_B \otimes \mathrm{rightRes}\bigr).
4471	  \]
4472	  \begin{proof}
4473	    \uses{lem:fbcb_gammaTopEquivEqLocus, lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
4474	    Tensoring the keystone isomorphism Lemma~\ref{lem:fbcb_gammaTopEquivEqLocus} by \(B\) over \(A\)
4475	    gives \(B \otimes_A \Gamma(X,M) \cong B \otimes_A \operatorname{eqLocus}(\mathrm{leftRes},\mathrm{rightRes})\),
4476	    and by flatness of \(B\) over \(A\) the Mathlib equivalence \(\operatorname{tensorEqLocusEquiv}\)
4477	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}) carries \(B \otimes_A (-)\) of the equalizer to the
4478	    equalizer of the base-changed legs. Composing the two yields the stated \(B\)-linear isomorphism.
4479	    By the same presentation applied to the pulled-back sheaf, the right-hand side is the global sections
4480	    of \(M\) over the base-changed scheme, so this is the module-level core of the \(H^0\)
4481	    flat-base-change comparison.
4482	  \end{proof}
4483	\end{lemma}
4484	
4485	\section{FBC-B: the global \texorpdfstring{$H^0$}{H0} flat-base-change isomorphism via the finite affine-cover equalizer (direct route)}
4486	
4487	The blocks of the preceding subsection assemble into a \emph{direct} proof of the named global
4488	isomorphism that bypasses the adjoint-mate keystone of the affine/mate route. The route is
4489	\emph{{\v C}ech-cohomology-free}: \(H^0\) is a finite limit — the equalizer of a finite affine cover —
4490	and the flat functor \(-\otimes_A B\) preserves finite limits, so the global comparison isomorphism
4491	follows from per-chart affine base change \emph{without} the abstract conjugate/mate identification and
4492	without the Mayer--Vietoris induction.
4493	
4494	Concretely, the module-level core
4495	\(B \otimes_A \Gamma(X,\mathcal{F}) \cong \operatorname{eqLocus}(\mathrm{id}_B\otimes\mathrm{leftRes},
4496	\mathrm{id}_B\otimes\mathrm{rightRes})\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:fbcb_baseChangeGammaEquiv}) already commutes flat
4497	base change past the finite \(H^0\) equalizer of a cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\). The single remaining step
4498	is to recognise the right-hand base-changed equalizer, chart by chart, as the equalizer-locus
4499	presentation (Lemma~\ref{lem:fbcb_gammaTopEquivEqLocus}) of the global sections of the pulled-back module
4500	over the base-changed cover \(\{(U_i)_B\}\) of \(X' = X_B\). The theorem below states that assembly.
4501	
4502	\begin{theorem}[Global \(H^0\) flat base change via the finite-cover equalizer, direct route]
4503	  \label{thm:fbcb_global_direct}
4504	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.baseChangeGammaPullbackEquiv}
4505	  \uses{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs, lem:fbcb_gammaTopEquivEqLocus,
4506	        lem:fbcb_baseChangeGammaEquiv, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso,
4507	        lem:affine_base_change_pushforward, lem:flat_base_change_reduce_global_sections}
4508	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 02KH (``Flat base change''), part (2), $i = 0$ case
4509	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L966--968).
4510	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\item if $S = \Spec(A)$ and $S' = \Spec(B)$, then
4511	  % $H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$."
4512	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KH (``Flat base change''), part (2), \(i = 0\) case.}
4513	  Let \(X\) be a quasi-compact, quasi-separated scheme with ground ring
4514	  \(A = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\), let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and
4515	  let \(B\) be a flat \(A\)-algebra. Write \(X' = X_B = X \times_{\operatorname{Spec}(A)}
4516	  \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with projection \(g' : X' \to X\) and \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) the
4517	  pullback. Then the comparison map
4518	  \[
4519	    \Gamma(X,\mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\; \Gamma(X', \mathcal{F}')
4520	  \]
4521	  is a \(B\)-linear isomorphism, assembled directly from the finite affine-cover equalizer presentation;
4522	  consequently the sheaf-level base-change map \(g^*(f_*\mathcal{F}) \to f'_*\mathcal{F}'\) is an
4523	  isomorphism, recovering the named global target Theorem~\ref{thm:flat_base_change_pushforward}. The
4524	  route uses neither the adjoint-mate keystone of the conjugate route nor the Mayer--Vietoris induction
4525	  on the number of cover members.
4526	
4527	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "\item We have $\psi^* \widetilde M = \widetilde{S \otimes_R M}$
4528	  % functorially in the $R$-module $M$." (references/stacks-schemes.tex, Tag 01I9, part (1) —
4529	  % the per-chart pullback-is-base-change identification feeding the assembly.)
4530	  \begin{proof}
4531	    \uses{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs, lem:fbcb_gammaTopEquivEqLocus,
4532	          lem:fbcb_baseChangeGammaEquiv, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso,
4533	          lem:affine_base_change_pushforward, lem:flat_base_change_reduce_global_sections}
4534	    Choose a finite affine cover \(\{U_i\}_{i\in\iota}\) of \(X\) with quasi-compact pairwise overlaps
4535	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}), so that \(\bigvee_i U_i = \top\).
4536	
4537	    \emph{Flat base change past the finite equalizer.} By
4538	    Lemma~\ref{lem:fbcb_baseChangeGammaEquiv}, flatness of \(B\) over \(A\) gives a \(B\)-linear
4539	    isomorphism
4540	    \[
4541	      B \otimes_A \Gamma(X,\mathcal{F}) \;\cong\;
4542	        \operatorname{eqLocus}\bigl(\mathrm{id}_B\otimes\mathrm{leftRes},\ \mathrm{id}_B\otimes\mathrm{rightRes}\bigr),
4543	    \]
4544	    where \(\mathrm{leftRes}, \mathrm{rightRes} : \prod_i \Gamma(U_i,\mathcal{F}) \rightrightarrows
4545	    \prod_{i,j}\Gamma(U_i\cap U_j,\mathcal{F})\) are the two restriction legs of the cover.
4546	
4547	    \emph{Per-chart identification of the base-changed legs.} The base-changed cover
4548	    \(\{(U_i)_B\}_{i\in\iota}\) is again a finite affine cover, now of \(X'\): base change preserves
4549	    affineness and open immersions and carries \(\bigvee_i U_i = \top\) to \(\bigvee_i (U_i)_B = \top\).
4550	    On each chart the affine pullback dictionary (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}, Stacks
4551	    Tag 01I9: \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^*\widetilde M \cong \widetilde{B\otimes_A M}\)) reads the
4552	    sections of \(\mathcal{F}'\) over \((U_i)_B\) as the base change of the sections of \(\mathcal{F}\),
4553	    \[
4554	      \Gamma\bigl((U_i)_B, \mathcal{F}'\bigr) \;\cong\; \Gamma(U_i,\mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B,
4555	    \]
4556	    and likewise on each overlap, where the affine base-change lemma
4557	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}) supplies
4558	    \(\Gamma((U_i\cap U_j)_B, \mathcal{F}') \cong \Gamma(U_i\cap U_j,\mathcal{F})\otimes_A B\). These
4559	    identifications are natural in restriction, so they intertwine the base-changed legs
4560	    \(\mathrm{id}_B\otimes\mathrm{leftRes}\), \(\mathrm{id}_B\otimes\mathrm{rightRes}\) with the
4561	    restriction legs \(\mathrm{leftRes}'\), \(\mathrm{rightRes}'\) of the cover \(\{(U_i)_B\}\) of
4562	    \(X'\); hence they restrict to an isomorphism of equalizer loci
4563	    \[
4564	      \operatorname{eqLocus}\bigl(\mathrm{id}_B\otimes\mathrm{leftRes},\ \mathrm{id}_B\otimes\mathrm{rightRes}\bigr)
4565	      \;\cong\;
4566	      \operatorname{eqLocus}\bigl(\mathrm{leftRes}',\ \mathrm{rightRes}'\bigr).
4567	    \]
4568	
4569	    \emph{Recognising the right-hand equalizer.} Applying the equalizer-locus presentation
4570	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:fbcb_gammaTopEquivEqLocus}) to \(\mathcal{F}'\) and the cover \(\{(U_i)_B\}\) of
4571	    \(X'\) identifies the latter equalizer locus with the global sections,
4572	    \(\operatorname{eqLocus}(\mathrm{leftRes}',\mathrm{rightRes}') \cong \Gamma(X',\mathcal{F}')\).
4573	    Composing the three isomorphisms yields the \(B\)-linear isomorphism
4574	    \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{F})\otimes_A B \cong \Gamma(X',\mathcal{F}')\), which is the comparison map.
4575	
4576	    \emph{Back to the sheaf-level statement.} Finally, the reduction
4577	    Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_base_change_reduce_global_sections} — each pushforward is the tilde of its module
4578	    of global sections, and pullback along \(g\) is \(-\otimes_A B\) on modules — turns this module
4579	    isomorphism into the isomorphism of the sheaf-level base-change map
4580	    \(g^*(f_*\mathcal{F}) \to f'_*\mathcal{F}'\), the named global target
4581	    Theorem~\ref{thm:flat_base_change_pushforward}. No conjugate/mate identification and no {\v C}ech
4582	    cohomology beyond the degree \(0/1\) sheaf condition enters.
4583	  \end{proof}
4584	\end{theorem}
